unitTitle: Unit 16: Materials and the Built Environment
title: Materials and the Built Environment

phrasal_verbs.meaning: cover a window or door with wooden boards
lock the doors of a building or business
be removed from something such as clothing or cloth by washing or rubbing; have a particular result or end in a particular way
remove something from a larger piece by cutting; stop eating something or doing something, especially because it is bad for your health
if something piles up or someone piles it up, the amount of it increases a lot
stop something from falling by putting something under it or against it; help a government, system, organisation, etc continue to exist, especially by providing financial or military support
fix something such as equipment in the place where it will be used and make it ready to use; make an official request, claim, offer, etc
make something by joining all its parts; produce or organise something using many different things; choose people or things to form a team or group
build something such as a wall, fence or house; let someone stay in your house
build a structure or put it in a particular place; make a piece of equipment ready for use
if people in a group spread out, they move away from one another so that they cover a large area
separate a large structure into pieces; write down information or a statement
add water to a drink or liquid in order to make it less strong, dilute; make something such as a statement or newspaper article less offensive, powerful or detailed
make something gradually disappear or become thinner by using or rubbing it; make someone gradually lose their energy or confidence

idioms.meaning: something that is ugly and spoils the appearance of a place
a city or area that has a lot of large ugly buildings and no trees or plants
behave in a relaxed way, as if you were in your own home
go to places of entertainment, such as bars and restaurants, in the evening
the area around a city where many people live who travel to the city to work
living in the countryside, far from towns or cities
a small town that regularly has a market and acts as a centre for the surrounding area
a place where you feel as relaxed and comfortable as in your own home
become homeless and have nowhere to live
said when you return to your home and are happy to be there
